trade.summary: Italia and Svezia have strong bilateral trade. Italia exports machinery, vehicles, food, and fashion; Svezia exports machinery, pharmaceuticals, vehicles, and paper products. Both are important EU manufacturing economies with complementary industrial strengths. Volvo trucks and Electrolux have significant Italian market presence.

diplomatic.summary: Italia and Svezia are EU and NATO allies (Svezia joined NATO in 2024). Diplomatic relations are warm and frequent within EU frameworks. Italia and Svezia differ somewhat on fiscal policy (Svezia tends toward fiscal conservatism) and migration. Svezia's new NATO membership has increased defense alignment with Italia.

military.summary: Both are NATO allies following Svezia's accession in March 2024. Italia supported Svezia's NATO application. Saab (Swedish) has some Italian defense market presence. Joint NATO exercises and interoperability. Both operate Gripen and Eurofighter-class aircraft (Italia flies Eurofighter; Svezia has Gripen).

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Swedish multinationals (Ericsson, ABB, Ikea, H&M, Volvo) have major Italian operations. Italian companies have some Swedish market presence. Strong corporate linkages through EU single market.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Some cooperation on energy transition. Svezia's excess renewable electricity and Italia's need for clean energy imports could create future linkages through European grid integration.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation through EU frameworks. Karolinska and Italian medical schools have exchanges. Joint research in life sciences, materials, and climate technology. Both countries are major contributors to EU Horizon research programs.
